created_by: v19 init Phase 2 bootstrap on 19 labeled examples (6 KCD + 13 INVESTIGATIVE_SURVIVING)

feature_descriptions.f1_jaccard_max_kcd: max Jaccard similarity (over all 6 KCD entries) between candidate.embedding_keys and entry.embedding_keys; range [0.0, 1.0]; high -> collision likely
feature_descriptions.f2_rare_math_count_in_kcd: count of candidate's rare-math vocab tokens that also appear in any KCD entry's embedding_keys; range [0, ~10]; high -> collision likely
feature_descriptions.f3_slot_in_productive_set: 1 if candidate.architecture_tool_slot in {S14, S15, S16, S20, S07}; 0 otherwise; 1 -> INVESTIGATIVE_SURVIVING likely
feature_descriptions.f4_citation_in_productive_set: 1 if any candidate.frontier_seed_citation in productive set; 0 otherwise; 1 -> INVESTIGATIVE_SURVIVING likely
feature_descriptions.f5_constraint_pattern_present: 1 if mechanism_signature contains constraint-pattern substring; 0 otherwise; 1 -> structurally fragile (R895/R920-style); higher collision/FAIL risk

weights_refit_note: Post-E38 refit on 26 labels (19 bootstrap + 7 new INVESTIGATIVE_SURVIVING from E38; 0 new EXTERNAL_COLLISION). The fit slightly increased w1 (Jaccard) and w2 (rare-math-count) toward more confident collision-class separation; slightly increased w3 and w4 magnitudes (productive-set membership stronger negative-class signal with more positive examples); reduced w5 magnitude (the E38 F5-positive R940 was a counterfactual FP, suggesting the constraint pattern's collision-prediction signal weakens with more INVESTIGATIVE_SURVIVING examples that lack f5=1).
label_convention: label=1 means collision/EXTERNAL_COLLISION (positive class for the classifier); label=0 means INVESTIGATIVE_SURVIVING (negative class for the classifier)

training_labels.note: E38 NEW: Bayesian-monad categorical critic head; ANCHOR_R834
E38 NEW: Voiculescu R-transform routing; sub-anchor of R843
E38 NEW: Bar-resolution cochain critic; ANCHOR_R863
E38 NEW: TTT meta-learned per-token adapter; ANCHOR_R883
E38 NEW: Power-law entropic loss reweighting; sub-anchor of R891
E38 NEW: Speicher S-transform routing; sub-anchor of R905
E38 NEW: Stochastic-visit-decay sparsity gate; ANCHOR_R922

last_agreement_rate_note: 7 of 7 rounds reaching step 14.6 in E38 had predicted_collision_prob < 0.3 (PASS) and step 14.6 verdict SURVIVES. Perfect agreement on the PASS-to-step-14.6 path. Counterfactual on REJECTED_LEARNED_VERIFIER (R934 TP, R940 FP) not included in this rate; tracked separately as TP/FP counts.
last_false_positive_rate_note: FP=1 (R940 counterfactual SURVIVES at sim=0.48); TN=7 (passed step 14.6 SURVIVES); FPR = FP / (TN + FP) = 1/8 = 0.125. Within target range 0.05-0.20.
last_true_positive_count_note: TP=1 (R934 counterfactual EXTERNAL_COLLISION at sim=0.74 vs arXiv 2511.18394 'Macaulay-Discriminant Token Routing'). The learned verifier correctly identified a Bezout-Macaulay-discriminant candidate as collision-risk before step 14.6 could fire. This is the v19 thesis validated: predictive pre-rejection avoids one full pipeline run's worth of computation AND prevents the KCD database from growing (database remains at 6 entries; if R934 had been allowed through, KCD would have grown to 7).

fit_diagnostics_post_E38.min_separation_margin_post_E38: 0.43 (KCD_R911 at 0.50) - (R895 at 0.14) = 0.36; margin widened by ~0.06 from bootstrap (0.30) due to new positive examples densifying productive-class cluster

honest_refit_note: Post-E38 refit: 26 labels (19 + 7 new INVESTIGATIVE_SURVIVING). Logistic regression with L2 regularization (lambda=1) fitted via gradient descent. Weights shifted slightly toward more confident class separation: |w_negative_indicators| increased (slot, citation more discriminative); w_f5 (constraint) magnitude reduced (the E38 R940 FP suggests constraint pattern is not strictly predictive of collision). Training accuracy maintained at 1.0. Min separation margin widened from 0.30 (bootstrap) to 0.36 (post-E38). The refit is deterministic given training data + lambda=1.

watch_anchors: ANCHOR_R908 (epochs_since_yield=1; sub-anchor of R863; watch for second-epoch yield)
